what_it_does: A React (Babel-in-browser style, no imports) screen component rendering a static Calendar view — a toolbar (Filter/Sort/Search/Clear/Task Type), a 'Top Tasks & Reminders' row of 8 task cards, a month navigator (June 2026), and a 7-day-by-3-week month grid with color-coded task chips. All content is placeholder/lorem data.
observations: Defines four function components: TopTask, CalChip, Day, and the main CalendarScreen
No import statements; relies on global JSX/React and exposes itself via window.CalendarScreen at the end
Uses cv- classes from platform.css (cv-screen-title, cv-toolbar, cv-btn--outline, cv-search, cv-cal-*) — purely a markup composition over the kit CSS
All data is hardcoded placeholder: 'Task Name', 'Due Date: 00/00/00', 'Lorem ipsum...', static day numbers 27..16
Color categories passed as string props (purple/green/blue/orange/yellow/gray/red) matching the chip/task color classes in platform.css
Inline SVG search-glyph uses var(--fg-muted) directly in stroke attributes
A couple of inline style objects (minWidth, font/color for the Manage link)
Emoji used as icons in markup (📊 Task Type, ‹ › ▾ chevrons)
Calendar grid is hand-laid as literal Day components, not computed from a date library
standouts: Entirely static mockup data — a visual scaffold, not a functioning calendar
Self-registration via window.CalendarScreen rather than ES module export
conventions: Follows the kit pattern of composing cv- classes rather than restating styles
Follows the browser-Babel / window-global mounting convention seen in claude-ds kits
Mixes a few inline styles into otherwise class-driven markup
concerns: Hand-laid calendar grid with literal day numbers will not reflect a real month/date
Repeated placeholder 'Task Name' / lorem text — clearly a non-functional mockup if mistaken for production
notes: A static placeholder Calendar screen for the platform kit — pure markup over platform.css classes, no real dates or data, registered on window.
questions: Is this meant to become data-driven, or to stay a fixed visual mockup?
Where does window.CalendarScreen get mounted (which host page consumes it)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches as a mockup — the filename and class usage say 'Calendar screen' and that is what it renders, though only as static layout with placeholder content.
intention_signals: Aims to show what the platform's Calendar screen looks like — toolbar, top-tasks strip, and a month grid with categorised task chips — as a recognisable visual for review.
contribution: Contributes the Calendar screen's layout and structure for the platform kit: the toolbar arrangement, the Top-Tasks strip, and the month-grid with color-coded task chips composed from kit classes.
summary_for_embedding: Static React Calendar screen for the platform UI kit — toolbar, top-tasks strip, and a month grid of color-coded task chips composed from platform.css cv- classes, registered on window.
intention_for_embedding: To present the platform Calendar screen's visual layout for recognition and review.
